prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: The third quarter of this strange annual cycle hath come to a close, and the scribes of this modern company of sorcerers have tallied their mystical figures. Verily, the number of users who have been ensnared by their digital wares hath grown by nigh on 15%, a figure of great import to these modern alchemists. Their treasure, which they call "revenue", hath swelled by a sum so vast it would make even the mightiest medieval king's coffers weep with envy. And yet, despite these great strides, the company's so-called "operating expenses" have also inflated, as if a great beast were continually devouring their hard-won gold. Truly, these modern times are full of strange and wondrous things that baffle the mind of this humble chronicler.
